title: carex serotina f. pygmaea
intro: Carex serotina f. pygmaea, also known as the dwarf woodland sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of flowering plant that grows in woodland areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The dwarf woodland sedge has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The plant can be used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by drying and processing the leaves and roots of the plant.
